about.bio: Machine Learning Engineer and Data Scientist with 2+ years of industry experience building and shipping ML/AI systems across NLP, Computer Vision, and predictive analytics. Skilled in Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Scikit-learn, with hands-on experience across the full model lifecycle: data ingestion, feature engineering, model training, evaluation, and deployment. Currently a graduate researcher at the Visual Computing and Biometric Security Lab (UNT), applying computer vision to Autism Spectrum Disorder detection. Published at EMNLP 2025 (top-tier NLP venue). Strong academic record (GPA 3.91, MS Data Science, University of North Texas). Seeking full-time roles in ML Engineering, Data Science, or Applied AI.

experience.title: Thesis Research — Visual Computing and Biometric Security Lab, UNT
Denton, TX | August 2025 – Present
details: Conduct computer vision research on Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) detection using deep learning models trained on behavioral and image-based datasets.
Design and evaluate CNN architectures for pattern recognition tasks relevant to ASD trait classification, contributing to an ongoing lab publication pipeline.
Apply transfer learning, data augmentation, and model interpretability techniques (Grad-CAM) to improve diagnostic transparency.
Collaborate with faculty and lab members on AI/ML research methodologies, experimental design, and result analysis.
Junior Machine Learning Engineer — Brain Station 23

experience.title: Dhaka, Bangladesh | Jan 2023 – Dec 2024
details: Built and deployed end-to-end ML pipelines for 5+ client projects spanning NLP, classification, and regression using Python, PyTorch, and Scikit-learn.
Processed and cleaned datasets with 100K+ records, addressing class imbalance, missing values, and feature drift to ensure reliable model inputs.
Trained and tuned supervised learning models including Random Forest, XGBoost, LSTM, and BERT; improved baseline accuracy by up to 15% through hyperparameter search and ensemble methods.
Built Tableau and Matplotlib dashboards to communicate model performance and business impact to non-technical stakeholders.
Collaborated closely with software engineers, product managers, and domain experts to align model outputs with real-world deployment requirements.
Student Assistant — University of North Texas

details: Digitized 161 analog infrastructure maps and visualized spatial data for CNG stations, industrial facilities, and captive power locations using QGIS.
Supported a government-sponsored digital transformation initiative under the Ministry of Power, Energy and Mineral Resources.
Teaching Assistant, Object Oriented Programming II (C#) — American International University Bangladesh

experience.title: Dhaka, Bangladesh | Jun 2021 – Aug 2021
details: Supported 80+ students on programming assignments and lab tasks in C#; provided hands-on help with Visual Studio, XAMPP, and GitHub.

projects.title: Clickbait Headline Detection — NLP / Web App, University of North Texas
Jan 2025 – Apr 2025
details: Built and compared three ML models for real-time clickbait detection: Logistic Regression (TF-IDF), LSTM, and fine-tuned BERT, achieving 99% classification accuracy with BERT.
Managed the complete pipeline from data collection and preprocessing to tokenization, training, evaluation (F1, precision, recall, AUC), and deployment.
Deployed the app using Streamlit with a REST API backend, enabling live inference on user-submitted headlines.
type:research
AI-Based Diabetic Retinopathy Detection — Computer Vision, University of North Texas

projects.title: Jan 2025 – Apr 2025
details: Designed and benchmarked EfficientNet-B0 and ResNet-50 for automated multi-class retinopathy detection from retinal fundus images using PyTorch.
EfficientNet-B0 reached 80% accuracy across 5 severity levels; applied transfer learning and data augmentation to handle class imbalance in the medical dataset.
Used Grad-CAM to generate saliency maps that highlight disease-relevant retinal regions, adding clinical interpretability to model predictions.
type:research
ASD Detection Research System — Computer Vision, UNT Lab

projects.title: Aug 2025 – Present
details: Designing CNN-based deep learning architectures for Autism Spectrum Disorder trait classification from behavioral and image-based datasets.
Applying Grad-CAM for model interpretability and transfer learning for improved generalization on limited medical data.
type:research

publications.title: Speaking at the Right Level: Literacy-Controlled Counterspeech Generation with RAG-RL
EMNLP 2025 — Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing (top-tier ACL venue)

publications.venue: • Built an LLM-based counterspeech generation system using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) to combat online hate speech with responses tailored to three user literacy levels.
• Built a hybrid model combining SVM and CNN to predict ASD traits from behavioral datasets, achieving 94% classification accuracy after a review of 25+ prior research papers.
